prose:
Fukui reactivity calculation on benzaldehyde completed using DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (tier standard, density-fitted) in PySCF, gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. The calculation computed Fukui indices (f⁺, f⁻, f⁰) and dual descriptors for all 14 atoms by taking differences in Mulliken charges between the neutral, cationic (+1, doublet), and anionic (−1, doublet) states. The most electrophilic site is carbon atom 3 (ortho-position to aldehyde), with f⁺ = 0.39938965 (reported to 8 significant figures as returned). The most nucleophilic site is the oxygen (atom 1), with f⁻ = 0.23839906. Both N±1 states converged; charge conservation checks passed (sum f⁺ = 1.0, sum f⁻ = 1.0). Full atom-by-atom Fukui indices, dual descriptors, and a reactivity visualization plot are in the result. Calculation is trustworthy and reproducible via the CLI invocation provided.</prose>
<parameter name="integrity_trustworthy">true
